author: conductor
title: Keep H11's server-side detach-spawn despite the lifted MCP-hook constraint
statement: H11 (note-structure extraction) STAYS on the server-side detach-spawn architecture even though the platform constraint that forced it — 'PostToolUse never fires on MCP tool calls' (CC 2.1.198, research_finding 5e7d0a78, Comsoft store) — is DISPROVEN on CC 2.1.215 by live probe (domain research_finding 72fa8697: PreToolUse and PostToolUse both fire on MCP tools, PostToolUse carries tool_response, and MCP-matched additionalContext injects). The lifted constraint is recorded knowledge, not a migration trigger. Hook-side MCP observation becomes a sanctioned OPTION for future designs that need it (e.g. the deferred delivered-evidence ledger behind the toothed gate, decision fe62546f) — each such design re-probes at build per the verify-at-build register (0956a464).

alternatives_rejected.option: Migrate H11 back to a registered PostToolUse hook now that the platform supports it
alternatives_rejected.reason: No current pain: the detach-spawn works, is mode-independent (immune to any residual headless/interactive hook quirks — issue #40506's class), and reverting is churn on a proven seam with zero outcome change (P1). The platform fact is volatile (changed at least once unlogged between 2.1.198 and 2.1.215); the server seam does not ride it.

alternatives_rejected.option: Leave the contradiction uninvestigated until H11 is next touched
alternatives_rejected.reason: The probe was nearly free (the harness existed from the H19 AC1 probe) and the contradiction was already surfaced — leaving a known docs-vs-KB conflict unresolved keeps the store lying (anti-drift).

rationale: Board 00845d07 disposition executed 2026-07-19: live probe (stdio MCP server + MCP-matched hooks, artifact evidence) resolved the docs-vs-KB contradiction decisively in favor of current docs. The architecture question the probe was gating is answered conservatively: keep what works, record what changed, let future needs opt in. Comsoft's store still holds the outdated 5e7d0a78 — supersede it from a Comsoft session; the promoted domain finding carries the correction to both projects meanwhile.
